title: Learning Graph Viewer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive vis-network viewer for exploring the ecology course learning graph, with search, category filtering, and prerequisite navigation.

educational.topic: Ecology Course Learning Graph and Concept Prerequisites
learningObjectives: Navigate the ecology learning graph to identify foundational concepts and their advanced dependents
Trace prerequisite chains to understand how early concepts support later topics
Identify clusters of related concepts that form major themes in ecology
Use the learning graph to plan study sequences and identify knowledge gaps
prerequisites: Enrollment in or familiarity with a high school ecology course
Basic ability to navigate interactive web applications
standards: NGSS Science and Engineering Practices: Developing and Using Models

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Self-directed graph exploration with metacognitive reflection
keyQuestions: Which foundational concepts support the most advanced topics in ecology?
How many prerequisites must be mastered before studying ecosystem services?
What does the structure of the learning graph reveal about how ecological concepts are interconnected?
How can the learning graph help you identify gaps in your understanding?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think ecology topics are independent rather than deeply interconnected through shared prerequisites
Many students believe they can skip foundational concepts and jump directly to advanced topics
Students may assume all concepts are equally difficult rather than recognizing that some require many more prerequisites
assessmentOpportunities: Evaluate student-identified prerequisite chains for completeness and accuracy
Assess personal study plans for logical sequencing based on the graph structure
Have students explain why certain concepts are foundational using evidence from the graph
